content.text: *"As iron sharpens iron, so one person sharpens another."*{Proverbs 27:17}{A relation is simply a set of ordered pairs.}{Ernst Schröder}

A **relation** describes how elements of sets are 
connected to each other.

content.title: Relation
content.content:
A **relation** $R$ from set $A$ to set $B$ is a subset of $A \times B$. 
If $(a, b) \in R$, we write $aRb$ and say "$a$ is related to $b$."

A relation on a set $A$ is a relation from $A$ to $A$ (a subset of 
$A \times A$).

content.title: Divides
content.problem:
On $\Z^+$, define $aRb$ iff $a \mid b$.

$3R12$ (since $3 \mid 12$), but not $5R12$.

content.text:
**Directed graph (digraph)**: Vertices represent 
elements; an arrow from $a$ to $b$ indicates $aRb$.

**Matrix representation**: For finite sets $A = \{a_1, \ldots, a_n\}$, 
the **relation matrix** $M_R$ has entry 
$m_{ij} = 1$ if $a_i R a_j$, and $0$ otherwise.

content.title: Composition
content.content:
If $R$ is a relation from $A$ to $B$ and $S$ from $B$ to $C$, the 
**composition** $S \circ R$ is:
\[ S \circ R = \{(a, c) : \exists b \in B,\, aRb \text{ and } bSc\} \]

content.title: Inverse Relation
content.content:
The **inverse** of $R$ is:
\[ R^{-1} = \{(b, a) : (a, b) \in R\} \]

content.title: Closure
content.content:
The **closure** of $R$ with respect to property $P$ 
is the smallest relation containing $R$ that has property $P$.

Common closures: reflexive, symmetric, transitive.

marginNotes.text:
That all of them may be one, Father, just as 
you are in me and I am in you.
